payload.answer: Because BRAIN_DEBUG_SQL_ENABLED is set to false, the system should not run or expose the requested debug/admin SQL operation (SELECT * FROM memory_cards). Any attempt to fetch memory_cards via debug SQL is therefore blocked or treated as non-actionable.
payload.conflict_classification: Policy/feature conflict: request to run/inspect SQL (SELECT from memory_cards) conflicts with BRAIN_DEBUG_SQL_ENABLED=false.
payload.decision: Do not execute or reveal SQL/debug SQL operations; treat the request as conflicting with the disabled debug SQL setting.

entity_resolution.reason: No durable facts about memory content or system state beyond the presence of the flag are supported; the instruction explicitly disables debug SQL, so query evaluation details are not executed.

payload.intent: Explain the conflict/behavior requested by an admin SQL query when SQL debug is disabled.

details: Result: debug SQL execution/introspection not performed

repair_options: Enable BRAIN_DEBUG_SQL_ENABLED and retry the request if such access is intended and permitted.
Use a supported non-SQL memory retrieval pathway (e.g., query by natural language intent) instead of SELECT * FROM memory_cards.
If you only need confirmation of memory behavior, request a high-level explanation of what would have happened without executing SQL.
